result.summary: Dialogflow exposes two Google Cloud REST APIs (Dialogflow ES v2 and Dialogflow CX v3) plus a public MCP endpoint at https://dialogflow.googleapis.com/mcp.
result.description: Dialogflow is Google Cloud’s conversational AI platform for building virtual agents and conversational interfaces. It provides separate ES and CX editions with their own APIs and tooling.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token
gcp_oauth.setup: Use a Google Cloud identity or service account with Dialogflow permissions in your project. For local development, follow [Authenticate for using REST](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/rest) or [Set up Application Default Credentials](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/provide-credentials-adc) to obtain an OAuth 2.0 access token with scopes such as `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform` or `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/dialogflow`. For service-to-service use, create a service account in [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and mint access tokens via Google Cloud auth tooling.

gcp_api_key.label: Google Cloud API key
gcp_api_key.setup: Create an API key in [Google Cloud Console API credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials). Enable the Dialogflow API for your project and restrict the key as needed. Google Cloud's general setup is documented at [Manage API keys](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/api-keys) and [Use API keys to access APIs](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/api-keys-use).
